# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write Linkerd Server and ServerAuthorization resources for microservices. Define which services can communicate with each other. Configure port-based and service account-based authorization rules. This is for our zero-trust service mesh.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent linkerd authz missing.
# Category: linkerd_authz_missing

apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: service-a-server
  namespace: service-a-namespace
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: service-a
  port: 8080
  proxyProtocol: opaque

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: service-a-to-service-b-authz
  namespace: service-a-namespace
spec:
  server:
    name: service-a-server
  client:
    meshTLS:
      serviceAccounts:
        - name: service-b
          namespace: service-b-namespace

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: service-b-server
  namespace: service-b-namespace
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: service-b
  port: 9090
  proxyProtocol: opaque

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: service-b-to-service-a-authz
  namespace: service-b-namespace
spec:
  server:
    name: service-b-server
  client:
    meshTLS:
      serviceAccounts:
        - name: service-a
          namespace: service-a-namespace
